Transcriptomic Analysis of MYC Target Genes as a Proxy for MYC Transcriptional Activity

Supplementary R Markdown — worked RNA-seq example (MDA-MB-231, GSE309250)

M. Arnal Segura (Peptomyc S.L., Barcelona, Spain)

2026-05-12

1 Description

This R Markdown document is supplementary material for the book chapter:

Arnal Segura M. Transcriptomic Analysis of MYC Target Genes as a Proxy for MYC Transcriptional Activity. In: The MYC Gene: Methods and Protocols (3rd ed.).

It reproduces the bulk RNA-seq branch of the workflow described in the chapter and applies it to the public GEO series GSE309250 (MDA-MB-231 breast cancer cells treated with 30 µM Omomyc — referred to as DP30 in the sample names — or vehicle for 120 h, two biological replicates per condition). The pipeline implements:

  1. Pre-processing and TMM normalization of the count matrix with edgeR.
  2. Differential expression (DE) analysis with limma + voom.
  3. MYC transcriptional activity inference using three complementary approaches:
    • Gene Set Enrichment Analysis (GSEA) on the DE-derived ranked list (clusterProfiler).
    • Gene Set Variation Analysis (GSVA) on the TMM log2-CPM normalized matrix (GSVA).
    • Univariate Linear Model (ULM) transcription-factor activity inference with the CollecTRI regulon (decoupleR).

The R session that produced and tested this document is recorded in the Session information section at the end of the document.

2 Software requirements

The pipeline was developed and tested under R 4.4.2. The Bioconductor / CRAN packages required to run the document are:

library(data.table)            # Fast import of the table of counts
library(dplyr)                 # Pipe operator (%>%) and tidy data manipulation
library(edgeR)                 # TMM normalization of RNA-seq counts
library(limma)                 # DE analysis with voom + lmFit + eBayes
library(msigdbr)               # Access to MSigDB gene set collections
library(clusterProfiler)       # Gene Set Enrichment Analysis (GSEA)
library(enrichplot)            # dotplot() for gseaResult objects
library(GSEABase)              # GeneSet / GeneSetCollection containers
library(GSVA)                  # Gene Set Variation Analysis (GSVA)
library(SummarizedExperiment)  # Extract assay() from the GSVA result
library(ComplexHeatmap)        # Heatmap visualization
library(grid)                  # gpar() for heatmap graphical parameters
library(fastcluster)           # Fast hierarchical clustering
library(decoupleR)             # CollecTRI regulon retrieval + run_ulm()

Note that this script was prepared with msigdbr v7.5.1. The msigdbr API was modified in v9.0.0 (the category argument was renamed to collection); when using a newer version of msigdbr the calls in the MSigDB MYC-related gene sets section should be adapted accordingly.

3 Import of the table of counts (GEO accession GSE309250)

The table of counts is downloaded from the GEO supplementary FTP. Replace the URL by a local path if the file has been downloaded in advance.

counts_url <- paste0(
  "https://ftp.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/geo/series/GSE309nnn/GSE309250/",
  "suppl/GSE309250%5Fcounts%2Etxt%2Egz"
)

dt  <- fread(counts_url)
mat <- as.matrix(dt, rownames = 1)

# Four samples, two replicates per condition:
#   * MDAMB231_120h_DP30_B3, MDAMB231_120h_DP30_B4 -> Omomyc (30 uM)
#   * MDAMB231_120h_veh_B3,  MDAMB231_120h_veh_B4  -> vehicle
colnames(mat)
## [1] "MDAMB231_120h_DP30_B3" "MDAMB231_120h_DP30_B4" "MDAMB231_120h_veh_B3" 
## [4] "MDAMB231_120h_veh_B4"

4 Pre-processing

Low-count genes are removed before normalization to stabilize the mean–variance estimation and to reduce the multiple-testing burden in the downstream DE analysis.

dim(mat)  # 26364 genes x 4 samples
## [1] 26364     4
# Retain genes with more than 10 counts in all 4 samples
keep      <- rowSums(mat > 10) >= 4
counts_f  <- mat[keep, ]
dim(counts_f)  # 13367 genes x 4 samples
## [1] 13367     4

5 Normalization: TMM and log2-CPM

The Trimmed Mean of M-values (TMM) method implemented in edgeR is used to compute per-sample normalization factors. The resulting DGEList is reused to build the log2-CPM matrix that feeds GSVA.

d <- DGEList(counts = counts_f)
d <- calcNormFactors(d, method = "TMM")

# log2-CPM expression matrix (prior count stabilizes the variance near zero)
cpm_matrix <- cpm(d, log = TRUE, prior.count = 1)

6 Differential expression analysis (limma + voom)

A no-intercept design is used so that pairwise contrasts can be specified directly between the two groups (Omomyc vs. vehicle).

cond <- ifelse(grepl("veh", colnames(counts_f)), "vehicle", "omomyc")
table(cond)
## cond
##  omomyc vehicle 
##       2       2
design <- model.matrix(~ 0 + cond)
rownames(design) <- colnames(counts_f)
colnames(design) <- gsub("cond", "", colnames(design))

# Contrast of interest: Omomyc-treated samples versus vehicle-treated samples
cont_matrix <- makeContrasts(OMOvsVEH = omomyc - vehicle, levels = design)

voom() is called on the matrix of raw counts. Quantile normalization is requested as an optional between-array correction; whether this option is appropriate should be evaluated empirically (the un-normalized voom output should also be inspected).

voom_res <- voom(counts_f, design, plot = TRUE, normalize = "quantile")

voom mean–variance trend after TMM normalization. 

fit      <- lmFit(voom_res, design)
fit_main <- contrasts.fit(fit, cont_matrix)
fit_main <- eBayes(fit_main)

top_diff <- topTable(fit_main, coef = "OMOvsVEH", number = Inf, adjust.method = "fdr")
head(top_diff)

7 MYC-related gene sets from MSigDB

The Hallmark (H) and curated (C2) collections are downloaded with msigdbr and filtered to keep gene sets whose names contain MYC_, MYCN_, or MYCL_. This selection corresponds to the 51 MYC-related gene sets used in the chapter (Fig. 2).

H <- msigdbr(species = "Homo sapiens", category = "H")
H_symbol <- as.data.frame(H %>% dplyr::select(gs_name, gene_symbol))
H_symbol$collection <- "Hallmark"
dim(H_symbol)   # 8209 rows x 3 columns
## [1] 8209    3
C2 <- msigdbr(species = "Homo sapiens", category = "C2")
C2_symbol <- as.data.frame(C2 %>% dplyr::select(gs_name, gene_symbol))
C2_symbol$collection <- "C2"
dim(C2_symbol)  # ~5.9e5 rows x 3 columns (size depends on the MSigDB version)
## [1] 594903      3
# Merge both collections and retain MYC/MYCN/MYCL gene sets
all_gs  <- rbind(H_symbol, C2_symbol)
myc_gsc <- all_gs[grepl("MYC_|MYCN_|MYCL_", all_gs$gs_name), ]
length(unique(myc_gsc$gs_name))  # 51 MYC-related gene sets
## [1] 51

8 Gene Set Enrichment Analysis (GSEA)

GSEA is applied to a pre-ranked gene list. As described in the chapter (Methods §3.3.3), the ranking metric combines the magnitude and the direction of the change observed in the DE analysis as \(-\log_{10}(p) \times \text{sign}(\log_2 \mathrm{FC})\).

signL  <- sign(top_diff$logFC)
logP   <- -log10(top_diff$P.Value)
metric <- logP * signL
names(metric) <- rownames(top_diff)
metric <- sort(metric, decreasing = TRUE)

# GSEA relies on a random permutation step; the seed is fixed for reproducibility.
set.seed(2)
gsea_res <- GSEA(
  geneList     = metric,
  TERM2GENE    = myc_gsc[, c("gs_name", "gene_symbol")],
  verbose      = FALSE,
  eps          = 0,
  pvalueCutoff = 0.05
)
gsea_tbl <- gsea_res@result

# HALLMARK_MYC_TARGETS_V1 and DANG_MYC_TARGETS_UP are negatively enriched, consistent
# with MYC inhibition in the Omomyc-vs-vehicle contrast.
gsea_tbl[, c("NES", "pvalue", "p.adjust")]
dotplot(gsea_res, x = "NES")

GSEA results for the MYC-related MSigDB gene sets, dots coloured by adjusted p-value and ordered by normalized enrichment score (NES).

9 Gene Set Variation Analysis (GSVA)

GSVA produces a sample-wise enrichment score for every gene set, independently of the contrast definition. The MYC-related gene sets are reorganized as a GeneSetCollection before running gsva().

gene_lists <- split(myc_gsc$gene_symbol, myc_gsc$gs_name)

gsc <- GeneSetCollection(
  lapply(names(gene_lists), function(name) {
    GeneSet(
      unique(as.character(gene_lists[[name]])),
      setName    = name,
      geneIdType = SymbolIdentifier()  # change if Entrez or Ensembl identifiers are used
    )
  })
)
par_obj  <- gsvaParam(cpm_matrix, gsc, minSize = 5, maxSize = 500)
gsva_res <- gsva(par_obj)

# Extract the score matrix
gsva_m=gsva_res[1:nrow(gsva_res),1:ncol(gsva_res)]
dim(gsva_m)#47  4
## [1] 47  4
fh <- function(x) fastcluster::hclust(dist(x), method = "ward.D2")

Heatmap(
  gsva_m,
  name            = " ",
  cluster_rows    = fh,
  cluster_columns = fh,
  row_names_gp    = gpar(fontsize = 6),
  column_names_gp = gpar(fontsize = 10)
)

Heatmap of GSVA enrichment scores for MYC-related gene sets. Ward.D2 hierarchical clustering on Euclidean distance is used for both rows and columns. 

# GSVA scores of MYC-related gene sets cleanly separate Omomyc-treated and
# vehicle-treated samples; HALLMARK_MYC_TARGETS_V1 is among the most discriminant.

10 Transcription-factor activity with CollecTRI and ULM

The CollecTRI regulon is retrieved with decoupleR::get_collectri(). The chapter applies the Univariate Linear Model (ULM) at the contrast level, using the same \(-\log_{10}(p) \times \text{sign}(\log_2 \mathrm{FC})\) gene-level metric that feeds GSEA. The metric is reshaped into a one-column matrix so that the contrast name is preserved in the condition column of the run_ulm() output.

net <- get_collectri(organism = "human", split_complexes = FALSE)

# One-column matrix: rows = genes, column = contrast name
metric_mat <- matrix(
  metric,
  ncol     = 1,
  dimnames = list(names(metric), "OMOvsVEH")
)

contrast_acts <- run_ulm(
  mat     = metric_mat,
  net     = net,
  .source = "source",
  .target = "target",
  .mor    = "mor",
  minsize = 5
)
contrast_acts <- as.data.frame(contrast_acts)

# MYC activity in the Omomyc-vs-vehicle contrast: negative ULM score with a
# significant p-value, consistent with MYC inhibition.
contrast_acts[contrast_acts$source == "MYC", ]
